claims.risk_if_overstated: Would imply more runtime authority than the recorded consult responses support.
claims.safe_public_wording: The recorded consult responses for this proof report pond-backed adapter status.

claims.risk_if_overstated: Would confuse a compact learning artifact with persistent general memory.
claims.safe_public_wording: A compact learning record was created for proof_015_learning_001.

claims.risk_if_overstated: Would treat source refs as proof of hidden state mutation.
claims.safe_public_wording: The learning record and consult responses preserve public-safe source refs and runtime hashes.

claims.risk_if_overstated: Would count copied text as replay.
claims.safe_public_wording: The repeat request includes a learning id and original solution hash but not the full first-pass solution.

claims.risk_if_overstated: Would claim a stronger recall mechanism than the visible artifacts prove.
claims.safe_public_wording: The repeat solution used the three-layer pattern after the replay response returned the learning record as a lineage ref.

claims.risk_if_overstated: Would turn a single artifact-bundle result into a broader performance claim.
claims.safe_public_wording: Within this proof bundle, the repeat pass was more actionable and preserved the required boundaries.
The proof does not assert global superiority.

claims.risk_if_overstated: Would imply access to private or hidden process data.
claims.safe_public_wording: The proof reports only public-safe artifacts, source refs, and hashes.

claims.risk_if_overstated: Would hide unresolved pressure from the replay response.
claims.safe_public_wording: The proof keeps uncertainty and pressure refs visible.
